suggestions.title: App.jsx is 900+ lines — extract components into separate files
suggestions.description: App.jsx contains WorkerCard, ProgressBar, StatusBadge, DeltaBadge, ActivityLog, ActivityLogEntry, StatsBar, and the root App. Extract WorkerCard+ProgressBar+StatusBadge to src/components/WorkerCard.jsx and ActivityLog+ActivityLogEntry to src/components/ActivityLog.jsx. This is a maintenance timebomb that compounds with every feature.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard back-face uses key={i} — unstable React keys
suggestions.description: App.jsx workerHistory.map((e, i) => <div key={i}>) uses array index as key. If activity entries change order, React will reuse wrong DOM nodes. Use key={e.timestamp + (e.type ?? '') + (e.worker ?? '')} for stable identity.

suggestions.title: No React ErrorBoundary — render errors white-screen the app
suggestions.description: A null reference in any component (e.g. malformed workers.json pushing null into CharacterAvatar) silently white-screens the entire app with no recovery. Add a class-based ErrorBoundary around the main content with a friendly fallback UI showing a reload button.

suggestions.title: Confetti setTimeout has no cleanup ref — timer stacking on rapid vibe changes
suggestions.description: App.jsx setTimeout(() => setConfettiActive(false), 2500) stores no ref. Rapid vibe rank changes stack multiple timers that toggle confetti out of order. Fix: use useRef to store timer ID, clear before scheduling new one, and clear in effect cleanup.

suggestions.title: Mobile office floor is cramped — characters overlap on 375px viewport
suggestions.description: On mobile (375px), the office floor characters overlap each other and desk labels are barely readable. The desk grid uses percentage positioning that doesn't scale well below 480px. Consider switching to a simplified list view on mobile or increasing the floor min-height.

suggestions.title: Activity log re-renders all entries on every poll cycle
suggestions.description: ActivityLog receives the full entries array prop which changes reference on every fetch (new array from JSON.parse). The .reverse().slice(0,20) creates new arrays each render. Memoize the displayed list with useMemo keyed on entries.length + last timestamp to avoid re-rendering 20 ActivityLogEntry components every 30s.

suggestions.title: Dashboard tabpanel only renders active tab — AT can't discover all panels
suggestions.description: WAI-ARIA authoring practices recommend rendering both tabpanels in the DOM with hidden attribute on inactive ones, so screen readers can understand the full widget structure. Currently only one tabpanel exists at a time.

suggestions.title: Ghost workers (OOO) have no visual distinction from active workers at a glance
suggestions.description: On the office floor, ghost/OOO workers look similar to active workers until you hover. Add a subtle opacity reduction (0.4-0.5) and a dashed ring around their avatar to make OOO status immediately obvious without interaction.

suggestions.title: No loading skeleton on initial fetch — blank cards flash before data arrives
suggestions.description: When the page first loads, there's a brief flash of empty state before workers.json loads. Add a simple skeleton/shimmer placeholder for the worker cards and activity log that shows during the initial fetch.

suggestions.title: App.css is 980+ lines — split into per-component CSS files
suggestions.description: App.css contains styles for every component. As the codebase grows, this becomes unmaintainable. When extracting components, co-locate their CSS (WorkerCard.css, ActivityLog.css, etc.).

suggestions.title: Office.jsx WindowElement polls every 60s but only changes once per hour
suggestions.description: WindowElement runs setInterval every 60s to check the hour, but the hour only changes once per hour. Calculate ms until next hour boundary and use setTimeout instead of fixed-interval polling.

suggestions.title: Vacant desk question mark icon is barely visible on dark background
suggestions.description: The vacant desk shows a small '?' that blends into the dark office floor. Increase its opacity or add a subtle dashed border outline to make empty desks more visible as potential spaces.
